title: Etude Realiste by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Love Poetry Lines
content:
A Baby's feet, like sea-shells pink,
Might tempt, should heaven see meet,
An angel's lips to kiss, we think,
A baby's feet.

Like rose-hued sea-flowers toward the heat
They stretch and spread and wink
Their ten soft buds that part and meet.
meaning: "Etude Realiste" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a love and nature poem consisting of 45 lines. The poet explores themes of love and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "A Baby's feet, like sea-shells pink, Might tempt, should heaven see meet,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
